items.mistake: Using `print` for anything beyond a one-off script.
items.why: Print loses levels, timestamps, structure, context and destination control.
items.example:
```python
# Before
print(f"chunked {len(chunks)} chunks")
# After
logger.info("chunked %d chunks", len(chunks))
```

items.fix: Libraries only call `getLogger(__name__)`; the *application* owns config.
items.why: Library-side `basicConfig` fights the app's intended formatters/handlers and is invisible to debug.
items.example: Add `logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` to library `__init__.py` to avoid 'no handler' warnings.

items.why: f-strings format eagerly even when the log level is suppressed — wasted CPU on hot paths.
items.example: Logging DEBUG f-strings in a tight loop while DEBUG is off still costs the formatting.

items.fix: Redact known sensitive keys in a `Filter` or formatter; never log raw API keys, tokens, or user prompts that may contain PII.
items.why: Logs leave your system (vendor log stores, backups). Each accidental leak is a real incident.

items.mistake: Logging without correlation IDs in a concurrent system.
items.fix: Bind `request_id` (and `user_id`, `trace_id`) at request entry; emit on every log line.
items.why: Without correlation, log lines from concurrent requests interleave into noise.
